text: The process of superimposing a low-frequency signal on a high-frequency carrier wave is called:
explanation: Modulation: signal wave is combined with carrier wave. Necessary because low-frequency signals cannot be transmitted as EM waves efficiently over long distances.
name: Modulation Definition

text: In amplitude modulation (AM), the _____ of the carrier wave is varied according to the message signal:
explanation: AM: amplitude of carrier varies with message signal. Carrier frequency stays constant. Used in medium-wave radio broadcasting.
name: Amplitude Modulation

text: The bandwidth of an AM signal with message frequency \(f_m\) is:
explanation: AM bandwidth: \( 2f_m \). Sidebands at \( f_c \pm f_m \). For voice (up to 4 kHz), AM bandwidth = 8 kHz.
name: AM Bandwidth

text: In frequency modulation (FM), the _____ of the carrier is varied:
explanation: FM: instantaneous frequency of carrier varies with message signal amplitude. More noise-resistant than AM. Used for high-fidelity music broadcasting (88-108 MHz).
name: Frequency Modulation

text: The process of extracting the original message signal from the modulated wave is called:
explanation: Demodulation (detection): recovers original message from modulated carrier at the receiver. Reverse of modulation. AM demodulation uses envelope detector.
name: Demodulation

text: The maximum line-of-sight range for a transmitter antenna of height \(h_T\) is:
explanation: Line-of-sight (LOS) range: \( d_T = \sqrt{2R_E h_T} \). Total range (with receiver height \(h_R\)): \( d = \sqrt{2R_E h_T} + \sqrt{2R_E h_R} \).
name: Line-of-Sight Range

text: The sky wave propagation uses which layer of atmosphere:
options: Ionosphere (reflects radio waves back to Earth)
explanation: Sky wave propagation: radio waves (2-30 MHz) reflected by ionosphere → can reach beyond horizon. Used for short-wave (HF) radio broadcasting.
name: Sky Wave Propagation

text: Space wave propagation (used for TV and FM) requires:
options: Line-of-sight transmission (frequencies > 40 MHz pass through ionosphere)
explanation: Space wave: VHF, UHF, microwaves (>40 MHz) pass through ionosphere. Requires line-of-sight. Used for TV, FM, satellite communication. Range limited by Earth's curvature.
name: Space Wave Propagation

options: As high as possible (more signal, less noise)
explanation: SNR: ratio of signal power to noise power. Higher SNR → better quality communication. In dB: \( SNR_{dB} = 10\log_{10}(P_s/P_n) \). Must be >> 1 (>> 0 dB).
name: Signal-to-Noise Ratio

text: The modulation index \(m_a\) in AM is:
options: \( m_a = A_m/A_c \) (ratio of message to carrier amplitude, \( 0 \leq m_a \leq 1 \))
explanation: AM modulation index: \( m_a = A_m/A_c \). \( m_a > 1 \): overmodulation (distortion). \( m_a = 1 \): 100% modulation.
name: AM Modulation Index

text: Optical fibre communication works on the principle of:
options: Total internal reflection (light confined inside fibre core)
explanation: Optical fibre: light undergoes TIR inside core (high n) surrounded by cladding (lower n). Very high bandwidth, low loss, immune to EM interference.
name: Optical Fibre Principle

text: The carrier wave in communication is high frequency because:
options: High-frequency waves have shorter wavelength making antenna size practical (\( L_{antenna} \approx \lambda/4 \))
High frequency means more energy only
explanation: Antenna size \( \propto \lambda \propto 1/f \). For audio (20 Hz), antenna would need to be 3750 km! High frequency makes antenna practical. Also allows many channels.
name: Why High Frequency Carrier

options: Amplitude of pulses varies with signal
Width (duration) of pulses varies with signal amplitude
explanation: PWM: pulse width varies with signal amplitude. Used in motor control, switching power supplies, digital-to-analog conversion.
name: Pulse Width Modulation

text: The term 'bandwidth' in communication refers to:
options: Range of frequencies occupied by the signal
explanation: Bandwidth: range of frequencies in a signal or channel. Higher bandwidth → more information per second (Shannon's theorem: capacity \( = B\log_2(1+SNR) \)).
name: Bandwidth Definition

text: A geostationary satellite is used for communication because it:
options: Appears stationary over a fixed point on Earth (same period as Earth's rotation, T=24h)
explanation: Geostationary satellite: T=24h, at ≈36,000 km altitude, appears fixed → used for direct-to-home TV, weather monitoring, and long-distance communication without tracking antennas.
name: Geostationary Satellite in Communication
